title: x-mcp-provenance/v0
description: The provenance annotation object carried under the key 'x-mcp-provenance/v0' inside an MCP content item's _meta. See spec/convention.md sections 4 through 7. Consumers MUST ignore unknown fields and treat an unknown trust level as untrusted.

origin.description: Where the content came from (convention section 4).

trust.description: What the client enforces (convention section 4). An unknown value MUST be treated as untrusted.

source.description: Informational only. MUST NOT contain secrets and MUST NOT be used by the client for any trust decision.

fenced.description: True if the content body uses span-level fencing per convention section 7.

integrity.description: Content integrity block (convention section 6). Required for trusted content, optional otherwise.

hash.description: Base64 of the hash over the exact emitted content body, including any fence sentinels.

signature.description: Optional authenticity signature tied to server identity. Null until a key distribution mechanism exists (out of scope for v0).
